title: Concord.ai onboarding: throughput & latency synchronization
preview:
From: Samir Gupta <samir@concord.ai>
To: Irene Choi <irene@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 1 Jun 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Initial load test data and latency targets

Hi Irene / Ben / Monica,

Thanks again for the call this morning. As discussed, we're ready to run the first full acceptance pass for Concord.ai's query service. Below are the primary acceptance targets we need to validate during the onboarding run: 

- Throughput: sustained 120 requests/sec (avg) for 30 minutes with standard 50% query mix
- P95 latency: < 180 ms end-to-end
- Tail (99): < 500 ms (traffic bursts allowed during the first 10 minutes)
- Error budget: fewer than 0.5% 5xx or timeouts across run

I've attached the sample load profile and the dataset split we'll use (concord_onboard_load_plan.csv). We can run the test window any time during 2027-06-02 or 2027-06-03 morning PT. We will need a short-lived test API key (read-only) and a stable model variant pinned for the run — please confirm the variant name you want us to use and whether we should expect any quantized/backed model to be used.

Also attaching a short log extract showing current internal baseline (initial_latency_sample.log) so you can map our expectations.

Can you confirm the schedule and provide the test key?

Thanks,
Samir
Samir Gupta
Head of Platform Engineering | Concord.ai
Attach: concord_onboard_load_plan.csv, initial_latency_sample.log


From: Irene Choi <irene@redwood.ai>
To: Samir Gupta <samir@concord.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 1 Jun 2027 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Initial load test data and latency targets

Samir — thanks for the clear targets and artifacts.

We can support a run on either June 2 or 3 morning PT. Proposed window: 2027-06-03 09:00–10:00 PT. We'll provision a dedicated capacity slot on a 4-GPU pool and pin the model variant you used in the demo (redwood-10b-fp16). For this acceptance run we recommend:

- Warmup: 10 minutes at 30% load to populate KV cache and pre-warm kernels
- Ramp: linear ramp to 120 rps over 5 minutes
- Sustain: 30 minutes steady state
- Cooldown: 5 minutes

We will generate a short-lived API key scoped to this run and send it to Samir via a separate secure link (do not post keys in email). Ben — can you attach the run results template (xlsx) and the runbook we will follow? Monica — I'll need your help coordinating telemetry capture and the replay harness on Concord side.

On acceptance criteria: if p95 is between 180–220 ms we can still pass conditionally if errors are below budget and Samir confirms functional parity — we'll document that as an SLA exception with a follow-up improvement ticket. Anything beyond that would require immediate investigation and potential fallback to a lower-quantized variant.

Thanks,
Irene Choi
Solutions Lead, Customer Success | Redwood Inference
Attach: (none)

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
To: Irene Choi <irene@redwood.ai>, Samir Gupta <samir@concord.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 2 Jun 2027 08:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Initial load test data and latency targets — run artifacts and notes

Irene / Samir — adding a couple of practical notes from the runtime side before we lock the window: 

1) Model variant: we will pin redwood-10b-fp16 as Irene noted. There is a quantized v10q that reduces memory but may increase p95 by ~20–40ms depending on prompt lengths — we won't use that for the first acceptance run unless Concord asks for it.

2) Telemetry: please make sure Concord's replay harness preserves the original tokenization boundaries; our prefix caching semantics are sensitive to sequence fragmentation. If you can run the supplied load_plan CSV unmodified that will make our thresholds meaningful.

3) Run artifacts: attached run_results_template.xlsx — please populate request distribution, per-second error counts, and a p50/p95/p99 latency column for each 5-min bucket. We'll attach server-side traces and consolidated token-level cost after the run.

4) Fallback policy: if p95 breaches 220ms during steady state we will immediately trigger the fallback variant (redwood-10b-fp16-stable) and mark the run "conditional pass" pending post-mortem.

I'll be on call for the run and will publish the runbook to the Drive link Irene supplied.

Regards,
Ben Carter
Senior SRE, Inference Runtime | Redwood Inference
Attach: run_results_template.xlsx

Ben — can you attach the run results template (xlsx) and the runbook we will follow? Monica — I'll need your help coordinating telemetry capture and the replay harness on Concord side.


From: Samir Gupta <samir@concord.ai>
To: Irene Choi <irene@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>, qa-team@concord.ai
Date: Thu, 3 Jun 2027 16:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Initial load test data and latency targets — confirmation and run summary link

Irene / Ben / Monica — thanks for the plan and the runbook. Concord confirms the 2027-06-03 09:00–10:00 PT window. I've asked QA to run the harness exactly as provided; we'll hand off the replay CSV at 08:45 so you can sanity-check tokenization.

Samir (on behalf of Concord QA) will post the preliminary metrics to the Drive folder after the run; please expect the consolidated summary named concord_run_summary_20270603.pdf.

Two quick asks before we close the loop: 
- Please include a short note in the run summary about any kernel-level stalls or memory-growth observations (if present).
- If we end up in a conditional pass state, can we schedule a follow-up improvement sprint and assign a primary owner from Redwood?

Appreciate the speed here — looking forward to the run.

Best,
Samir
Samir Gupta
Head of Platform Engineering | Concord.ai
Attach: concord_run_summary_20270603.pdf

title: GCP partner opportunity alignment & joint workbench
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Priya Singh <priya.singh@google.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 1 Jun 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: GCP partner opportunity alignment & joint workbench

Hi all —

We have two priority GCP co-sell candidates this quarter (HelixBio and NurtureHealth) and I want to run a short joint workbench to align partner PMs, field, and our AE/CS owners on next steps. Goal is a tidy mutual action plan and a quick decision on which deal to prioritize for GCP co-sell nomination next week.

Proposed agenda (30-40 min):
- 5m: Introductions + objectives
- 10m: HelixBio opportunity summary (AE + partner PM)
- 10m: NurtureHealth snapshot and technical open items
- 10m: Prioritization & next steps (MAP, GCP kickoff, enablement)

Priya — are you available Wed/Thu this week for a 30 min sync? If you prefer, we can run separate 30 min workbenches for each account but I thought getting partner PMs in one room would speed decisions.

I've attached a short agenda and a starter MAP template. Please drop your availability and any additional artifacts you want reviewed.

Thanks,
Ben

--
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0123

Attachment: 20270601-gcp-workbench-agenda.pdf (PDF stub, 24KB)


From: Priya Singh <priya.singh@google.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 1 Jun 2027 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: GCP partner opportunity alignment & joint workbench

Thanks Ben — happy to join. Wed/Thu both work for me, but I prefer Thu afternoon PT to leave room for prep.

Quick notes before the session:
- Please share the current MAPs (Mutual Action Plans) and HubSpot record IDs so we can link co-sell nomination items to the right opportunities.
- I'll bring the GCP enablement checklist and a short overview of co-sell qualifying criteria (buying committee, security readiness, POC plan).
- If HelixBio has any compliance asks (SOC2 or specific network topologies) share them now so I can flag to our Solutions Architect.

Suggested slot: Thu 2027-06-03, 2:00–2:30 PM PT. If that works I'll send a calendar invite and add the relevant GCP SE.

Best,
Priya
Partner Manager, Google Cloud (GCP)
priya.singh@google.com

On Tue, Jun 1, 2027 at 09:12 AM Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi all —
> 
> We have two priority GCP co-sell candidates this quarter...


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Priya Singh <priya.singh@google.com>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 1 Jun 2027 12:07:00 -0700
Subject: Re: GCP partner opportunity alignment & joint workbench

Priya — Thu 2:00–2:30 PM PT works for us. Please go ahead and send the invite and include the GCP SE.

A couple of prep asks so we use the time efficiently:
- Vanessa/Marissa: please attach the current MAPs for HelixBio (HubSpot HSP-4589) and NurtureHealth (HSP-4594).
- Neha: can you list the outstanding technical blockers for NurtureHealth (POC data flow, network, and embedding latency expectations)?
- Karthik: recommend a short prioritization rubric (impact x probability) to help us pick one candidate for a co-sell nomination this cycle.

I attached the HelixBio MAP draft (HelixBio-MAP-v1.xlsx). Let's try to lock owners during the meeting.

Thanks — will look out for the invite.

Ben

Attachment: HelixBio-MAP-v1.xlsx (Excel stub, 72KB)

On Tue, Jun 1, 2027 at 11:03 AM Priya Singh <priya.singh@google.com> wrote:
> Thanks Ben — happy to join...


From: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Priya Singh <priya.singh@google.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 2 Jun 2027 08:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: GCP partner opportunity alignment & joint workbench

All — adding a quick priority ranking before the workbench so we can triage faster:

Priority 1: HelixBio (strong exec sponsor, three-week pilot budget, procurement started)
Priority 2: NurtureHealth (good fit but requires dataplane review + longer security review)

I uploaded an updated MAP for HelixBio with owner suggestions and timing (see attached). Key open items for HelixBio: POC timeline (target start 6/14), SOC2 evidence request (security), and a short list of trial success metrics.

I also pasted the HubSpot links below — please confirm records before the meeting so we sync to the right pipeline items.

- HelixBio (HubSpot HSP-4589)
- NurtureHealth (HubSpot HSP-4594)

Vanessa
Partner Ops Lead | Redwood Inference
vanessa@redwood.com

Attachment: HelixBio-MAP-v2.xlsx (Excel stub, 95KB)

On Tue, Jun 1, 2027 at 12:07 PM Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com> wrote:
> Priya — Thu 2:00–2:30 PM PT works for us...


From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa@redwood.com>, Priya Singh <priya.singh@google.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 3 Jun 2027 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: GCP partner opportunity alignment & joint workbench

Thanks team — calendar invite sent for Thu 2027-06-03 2:00–2:30 PM PT with GCP SE (Anil Rao) and HelixBio AE (Jason Li) included.

Action items ahead of the workbench:
- Ben / Jason (HelixBio AE): please prepare a 5-slide account snapshot focused on buying committee, POC hypothesis, and procurement timeline.
- Neha: bring the short tech blockers list and latency baseline measurements.
- Vanessa: we'll walk the MAP and lock owners during the session.

If HelixBio wants the technical deep dive, let's schedule a follow-up with Field Eng for Wed 6/16 (30–45 min).

See you Thursday.

Marissa
Director, Partnerships | Redwood Inference
marissa@redwood.com

On Wed, Jun 2, 2027 at 08:45 AM Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa@redwood.com> wrote:
> All — adding a quick priority ranking before the workbench so we can triage faster...

title: Vendor banking proof + remittance encoding (Verta Systems)
preview:
From: finance@verta-systems.com
To: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>
Cc: 
Date: Tue, 1 Jun 2027 09:12:00 +0100
Subject: Verta — onboarding forms + next steps for payments

Hi Aditya,

Thanks for the intro. I'm Sara in finance at Verta Systems. Attached are our completed vendor onboarding form and our bank letter — please confirm what additional evidence Redwood needs to enable EUR wires and SEPA credits to our account. We typically send PO numbers on the remittance in the following format: PO12345|INV-6789 (line-level tags).

Two quick asks upfront:
- Can you confirm whether Redwood can accept an XML remittance payload (we can provide a sample).
- Do you require a separate virtual-account mapping or will a single IBAN be sufficient for the first pay run?

Files attached: Verta_Onboarding_Form_20270601.xlsx, Verta_Bank_Letter_20270601.pdf

Thanks,
Sara Klein
Finance Manager
Verta Systems
finance@verta-systems.com



From: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>
To: finance@verta-systems.com (finance@verta-systems.com)
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 1 Jun 2027 10:01:00 +0100
Subject: Re: Verta — onboarding forms + next steps for payments

Sara — thanks, I have what you sent. Quick answers while Priya (FinOps) is looped:

- We can accept an XML remittance sample for mapping — please attach the file when you reply.
- For initial onboarding a single IBAN is fine; if you need per-PO virtual accounts later we can discuss VA mapping.

Priya — can you confirm the internal PO tagging requirement and whether we need a separate payee reference field in the remittance for line-level PO tags? Also, any required formatting (delimiter, max length)?

Aditya
Director, Partnerships Ops
Redwood Inference

On Tue, Jun 1, 2027 at 09:12, finance@verta-systems.com wrote:
> Hi Aditya,
> 
> Thanks for the intro... (original message attached)
>


From: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>, finance@verta-systems.com
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 1 Jun 2027 11:23:00 +0100
Subject: Re: Verta — onboarding forms + next steps for payments

Thanks, Sara — and thanks Aditya. Couple of operational notes from FinOps: 

1) Remittance format: we support an XML remittance payload and can map PO tags. For line-level POs we prefer a pipe-delimited field (e.g. PO12345|INV-6789) — that matches your example. Please keep each PO reference under 64 characters.

2) For EUR payments in EU region we do SEPA by default where available; for SWIFT wires we need the bank letter and an additional signed bank proof (we can accept a bank-attested PDF, which looks like the one you attached).

3) Test payment: we run a single test transfer (low-value wire/SEPA) prior to enabling live pay runs. Once we receive the signed onboarding form and the remit XML sample we will schedule the test (typical ETA 2 business days).

4) DocuSign: if you prefer, upload the onboarding form to DocuSign and send the link. I attached our internal guidance doc (not included here) but the steps are standard.

Marissa — can you confirm the PO -> invoice routing labels expected in accounting?

— Priya
Sr. Manager, FinOps
Redwood Inference

---
Priya Natarajan
(prior message quote)


From: Sara Klein <sara.klein@verta-systems.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>
Cc: 
Date: Wed, 2 Jun 2027 08:37:00 +0100
Subject: Re: Verta — onboarding forms + next steps for payments

Hi Priya / Aditya,

Thanks for the clarifications. I've attached an example remittance payload (verta_remit_sample.xml) and our signed onboarding form. We also added the bank-attested confirmation you requested (Verta_Bank_Letter_20270601.pdf).

A few notes on our side:
- We will include PO tags as POnnnnn|INV-mmm in the remittance (pipe-delimited).
- For the initial test, we prefer SEPA; if Redwood needs SWIFT for any routing edge-case please let us know.
- We can accept the test debit today between 10:00–14:00 CET if that helps accelerate activation.

Attachments: Verta_Onboarding_Form_20270601.xlsx (signed), Verta_Bank_Letter_20270601.pdf, verta_remit_sample.xml

Sara

On Tue, Jun 1, 2027 at 11:23, Priya Natarajan wrote:
> Thanks, Sara — and thanks Aditya. Couple of operational notes...
>


From: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>
To: Sara Klein <sara.klein@verta-systems.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 3 Jun 2027 11:45:00 +01:00
Subject: Re: Verta — onboarding forms + next steps for payments

Sara — got the signed form and remittance sample, thank you. Confirming next steps:

1) Priya's team will ingest the provided XML sample and map the PO tag to our invoice routing field (we'll keep the pipe delimiter).
2) We will schedule a single SEPA test transfer today at 13:30 CET into the IBAN you provided. Expect a low-value test (EUR 0.01–0.10) for confirmation.
3) After the test clears, we will flip your status to Active for scheduled pay runs; we will cc billing@verta-systems.com on the confirmation.

Marissa — please create the payee in our vendor portal with the attached bank-letter and onboarding workbook, tag account as "EU-SEPA", and include the PO label mapping.

Sara, I'll reply to this thread once the test has been initiated and provide the trace/reference. If anything changes on timing we'll escalate, but ETA remains 2 business days for full activation post-test.

Thanks,
Aditya Rao
Director, Partnerships Ops
Redwood Inference

preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>

Cc: 
Date: Fri, 28 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: SFDC: horizon clean & decommit guidance for Greenline Health

Team — quick note ahead of week-close. Finance asked us to clean up anything that looks like a soft commit in the current close window so forecast numbers are actionable. I pulled a short list of Greenline Health opportunities that are skewing our commit: see attached flagged-opps-may28.csv.

Ask: please take the actions below by EOD Tuesday (6/1):
- Verify the true customer intent for each opp in the CSV and update Stage to one of: Commit / Commit - At Risk / No Go.
- If no substantive buyer activity in 30+ days and no upcoming meeting, decommit (move to Closed - Lost or reclass to Prospect).
- If customer confirmed but needs paperwork or final pricing, set Close Date to the realistic quarter and add a <last_touch> note summarizing next step.

Three specific opps I flagged as higher priority:
- GL-2027-ACQ (Account expansion) — last activity 4/14; there was a demo but no pricing discussion.
- GL-2027-ANNUAL (renewal upsell) — procurement looped in; proposal sent 5/10, DocuSign pending.
- GL-2026-ENG (pilot) — stalled, no touch in 45 days; currently in Commit in SFDC.

If you want Finance to approve any short-term credit/terms to preserve commit, note it inline and cc: finance-ops. Otherwise we should be conservative and decommit where appropriate — we can't have phantom commit inflate next quarter.

Attachment: flagged-opps-may28.csv (columns: deal_id,owner,last_touch,stage,close_date,notes)

Thanks —
Ben

title: Vendr portal onboarding + SSO provisioning and evidence handoff
preview:
From: Kyle Ross <kyle.ross@acmetech.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: support@vendr.co
Date: Tue, 1 Jun 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Vendr onboarding: SSO & evidence upload — AcmeTech security review

Hi Ben,

Thanks for the intro. We're kicking off a security review at AcmeTech for a potential procurement decision and Vendr suggested we use their portal to share the CAIQ + SOC artifacts. Two quick asks to get the portal and SSO mapped: 

1) Can Redwood provide the Vendr portal admin contact (okta metadata / SAML entity) so we can map the AcmeTech Okta app to the right group?
2) We have a current CAIQ (attached) and a network diagram. What's the preferred evidence flow: upload directly to Vendr, or do you prefer a short-lived S3 presigned link?

We're also happy to sign an NDA if required (attached is our standard). Would love to lock a 30min handoff later this week to finalize. 

Attachments: acmetech_caiaq_2026.pdf (pdf), acmetech_network-diagram-merged.png (png), signed-nda-acmetech.pdf (pdf - unsigned placeholder)

Thanks,
Kyle Ross
Head of IT Security, AcmeTech
kyle.ross@acmetech.com
+1 (415) 555-0193

---
(note: original question also sent to Vendr support to ensure portal provisioning)

To: Kyle Ross <kyle.ross@acmetech.com>

Date: Tue, 1 Jun 2027 10:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Vendr onboarding: SSO & evidence upload — AcmeTech security review

Kyle — thanks for sending these over and for the clear asks. Quick plan to move fast: 

- Priyom (security) will own the artifact checklist and decide S3 vs portal upload.
- Laura (platform ops) will coordinate the Vendr admin mapping and Okta metadata exchange.
- I'll set the handoff call and confirm the NDA flow (we can do DocuSign).

For this to be smooth, please confirm the AcmeTech Okta metadata (entityID + ACS URL) or alternatively we can provide a Vendr SAML metadata file you can drop into your Okta app.

Proposed 30min windows for onboarding call: Thu 6/3 10:00-10:30 PT or Fri 6/4 13:00-13:30 PT. Which works for you?

I've attached a small Vendr invite/stub (Vendr portal typically requires the vendor to accept an invite before uploading evidence). I've CC'd Vendr support to trigger the portal provisioning if needed.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com

On Tue, Jun 1, 2027 at 09:12 AM Kyle Ross <kyle.ross@acmetech.com> wrote:
> Hi Ben,
> 
> Thanks for the intro. We're kicking off a security review...



From: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwoodinference.com>
To: Kyle Ross <kyle.ross@acmetech.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Vendr Support <support@vendr.co>
Date: Tue, 1 Jun 2027 14:21:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Vendr onboarding: SSO & evidence upload — AcmeTech security review

Kyle — thanks for the CAIQ and diagram. A few details from our side and a short artifact checklist to streamline ingestion: 

1) SSO / provisioning
   - Preferred: we provide a Vendr SAML metadata file (vendor side) for your Okta admin to import. If AcmeTech prefers to initiate, send entityID + ACS URL and we will create the Okta app.
   - Group mapping: create an Okta group named "acmetech_vendr_reviewers" and add the security reviewers; we'll map that to portal role "evidence_uploader".

2) Evidence flow (choose one):
   - Direct Vendr upload (recommended): portal link + invite (Vendr will notify us and we pull artifacts into our tracker)
   - S3 presigned: create a single s3 prefix and provide 72-hour presigned links for the files. We will download and ingest then delete.

3) Required artifacts for initial assessment (we can accept drafts):
   - Latest SOC 2 Type II summary (redacted ok)
   - CAIQ (received)
   - Network diagram with VPC/subnet boundaries (received)
   - KMS / key management description and contact
   - Data retention policy + encryption at rest proof (S3 bucket policy or KMS ARN)

4) Service account + API key: we would like a Vendr service account provisioned for Redwood with scoped read access so we can programmatically pull evidence going forward. If you prefer not to provision, we will rely on manual downloads.

5) NDA / legal: if you can send the signed NDA or a DocuSign link we will countersign within 24 hours.

Once we have those items we will do an initial pass and surface anything missing. If you want, Laura can run the portal mapping call and I will lead the artifact review.

Next steps: confirm preferred evidence flow and pick one of the proposed call slots (Thu 6/3 10:00 PT or Fri 6/4 13:00 PT).

Thanks,
Priyom Das
Security Engineer, Redwood Inference
priyom.das@redwoodinference.com

Date: Wed, 2 Jun 2027 11:35:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Vendr onboarding: SSO & evidence upload — AcmeTech security review

- Okta metadata: attaching our metadata XML and I've asked our Okta admin to create the group acmetech_vendr_reviewers and add the two reviewers listed below.
- Evidence flow: we'll do direct Vendr upload. Vendr support — please provision the AcmeTech vendor record and send the portal invite to kyle.ross@acmetech.com and to the group once available.
- Service account: we can provision a scoped API key for Redwood after the portal is live. Please send the minimum permissions required and we will create it.
- NDA: signed-nda-acmetech.pdf attached (signed by AcmeTech); please countersign via DocuSign link if Redwood needs a copy.

title: Demo recap + proposed trial sprint (evaluation gates & owners) - Cobalt Retail
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Jessica Park <jessica.park@cobaltretail.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-05-29T15:22:00-07:00
Subject: Demo recap + proposed trial sprint (evaluation gates & owners) - Cobalt Retail

Hi Jessica — thanks again for taking the demo today. Quick recap and a proposed lightweight trial/POC so we can validate fit without heavy ops overhead. Putting owners and timelines below so everyone has clarity.

TL;DR: We recommend a 3-week evaluation sprint focused on catalog search acceleration + query re-ranking. Success gate is a >=20% improvement in median query latency and parity on relevance (Precision@5 within 5% of current production).

Proposed evaluation sprint (3 weeks)
- Week 0 (kickoff): onboarding, test data ingestion, baseline metrics (Ben / Marissa)
- Week 1: parallel inference on 10k queries, latency profiling, first relevance pass (Vivek / Cobalt eng lead - Anil)
- Week 2: A/B comparison, iteration on prompts/quantization, handoff of evaluation report (Marissa / Jessica)

Success criteria (must-pass to move to pilot)
- Latency: >=20% reduction in median per-query latency at target throughput (specified below)
- Relevance: Precision@5 within -5% of current baseline OR qualitative approval from 3 buyer stakeholders
- Stability: <1% error rate over 24h steady-state testing

Logistics and throughput
- Target test throughput: 50 qps sustained, burst to 120 qps for spikes
- Data: 10k synthetic+real queries (we can take a sampled window you provide)
- Access: read-only S3 bucket + example query log (we requested sample during call)

Attachments: attached a 1-page scope (cobalt-trial-scope-v1.pdf) and an .ics for the tentative kickoff (redwood-poc-timeline.ics). Also included sample-output-snippets.txt to show expected JSON payloads.

Open questions / asks for Cobalt before kickoff
1) Confirm the engineering owner who will share the query logs and S3 access (Anil? procurement for approvals?)
2) Any compliance constraints (data residency or masking) we should bake into the trial config?
3) Preferred dates for Week 0 kickoff (we suggested June 7 or June 14).

Next steps (action items)
- Jessica: confirm data owner + procurement contact by EOD Tue (May 31)
- Anil: share a 48-hour sample query log (s3:// or secure transfer)
- Ben/Marissa: publish trial plan doc and small runbook (attached)

If that looks good I’ll lock in a kickoff and send calendar invites.

Thanks — Ben

--
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0192



From: Jessica Park <jessica.park@cobaltretail.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Anil Suresh <anil.suresh@cobaltretail.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-05-30T09:04:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Demo recap + proposed trial sprint (evaluation gates & owners) - Cobalt Retail

Thanks Ben — appreciate the clear plan. Quick answers inline and a couple of constraints from our side: we have strict PII handling and a procurement review window.

- Engineering owner: yes, Anil will be primary for data and integration. I’m looping in procurement (procurement@cobaltretail.com) for contract/NDAs.
- Compliance: we need PII redaction on any query text before you ingest — we can provide a redaction script or request Redwood run in a review-only mode.
- Dates: June 14 kickoff looks safer because procurement needs ~10 business days to approve the trial terms.

Success criteria: the latency objective looks right. On relevance we’ll accept a combined metric (Precision@5 + one manual QA pass). Also add an ownership column to the plan for who signs off the manual QA.

Attachments I’ll share: a sample anonymized query export by EOD Friday (June 4).

Confirming: move kickoff to the week of June 14 and we’ll target completion the week of July 5 if iterations are needed.

Jessica
Director, Product & Analytics
Cobalt Retail
jessica.park@cobaltretail.com



From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Jessica Park <jessica.park@cobaltretail.com>
Cc: Anil Suresh <anil.suresh@cobaltretail.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>, procurement@cobaltretail.com
Date: 2027-05-31T08:12:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Demo recap + proposed trial sprint (evaluation gates & owners) - Cobalt Retail

Perfect — appreciate the timeline and the compliance note. Updated plan below reflecting PII redaction, sign-off owners, and the June 14 kickoff. I also added a minimal rollback/fallback in case we exceed error thresholds during stress runs.

Updated sprint highlights (final)
- Kickoff: Tue June 15 (UTC-friendly times, calendar invite to follow)
- Deliverables: baseline report, 2 A/B runs, final evaluation deck + recommendations
- Sign-offs:
  - Data ready: Anil Suresh (Cobalt)
  - Manual relevance QA: Jessica Park + Product lead (named QA approver once proc review completes)
  - Pilot go/no-go: Cobalt executive sponsor (proc to include signatory)

Trial controls (operational)
- PII: Redwood will execute ingestion in review-only mode and apply the redaction script you provide. Ingress logs will never be persisted beyond the trial window (30 days retention).
- SLA: trial has no SLA but we’ll maintain a monitored runbook with paging to Vivek for infra issues.

Next steps (short list)
1) Procurement: procurement@cobaltretail.com to send any redlines for the trial terms (Ben will consolidate).
2) Anil: share anonymized sample export by June 4.
3) Ben/Marissa: send calendar invite for June 15 kickoff and the runbook doc (end of day June 1).

If we don’t hear procurement by June 4, we’ll still prepare the infra and hold the slot June 15; we can do a restricted dry-run with synthetic data until approvals are done.

Thanks — locking this in now.

Ben
--
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0192



From: procurement@cobaltretail.com <procurement@cobaltretail.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Jessica Park <jessica.park@cobaltretail.com>
Cc: Anil Suresh <anil.suresh@cobaltretail.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-06-02T11:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Demo recap + proposed trial sprint (evaluation gates & owners) - Cobalt Retail

Hi Ben / team — procurement here. Reviewed the trial terms and we have two small asks before we sign the trial agreement:
1) Add a one-page appendix that describes data handling and retention steps (we need explicit wording on in-transit encryption, S3 access control, and 30-day retention).
2) Liability cap for the trial: propose a mutual liability cap of $10,000 for trial activities.

Otherwise the scope looks fine and we can push the signature through within 5 business days after the appendix is attached. Please send a short appendix and I will circulate for countersignature.

Procurement
Cobalt Retail
procurement@cobaltretail.com

Hi Aisha,

Thanks again for the quick intro call yesterday. Were keen to get a weeklong exec workshop scheduled in San Francisco (Redwood office or nearby venue) in August — target week of Aug 9. High-level: 12-15 onsite execs + ~6 remote participants for daily panels.

Primary asks so we can start procurement:
- Confirm preferred week/dates (we have some internal travel windows)
- Hotel room block recommendations + approximate per-night budget (corporate rate)
- Whether Redwood can issue an invoice/PO to speed travel bookings or we should route through our procurement team
- AV requirements (mics, streaming setup, recording) and whether Redwood has a preferred AV vendor
- Visa/attendance letters for two APAC attendees (need invitation wording)
- Any standard COVID/vaccination or security entry checks?

Attaching our initial workshop requirements spreadsheet. Would appreciate a short run-through of proposed dates and an initial cost estimate so procurement can start a PO. Also please loop in whoever will be our onsite contact.

Thanks — look forward to aligning.

Best,
Julia
Julia Park
Head of Strategic Programs, ZenithAI
[Attachment: zenithai_requirements.xlsx (Excel, 42KB)]

From: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>

Date: 2027-06-01T09:30:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Redwood exec workshop — SF week options, AV & procurement needs

Hi Julia,

Fantastic — wed love to host. Helpful answers inline and next steps below.

Dates: Available windows at our SF office: Aug 9-13 OR Aug 16-20. Were proposing Aug 10-12 for a compact two full-day + half-day wrap (gives your team travel buffers). Please confirm preferred week by EOD Tuesday so we can lock venue and hotel blocks.

Hotel + travel: We can coordinate hotel blocks with our preferred partner (Hilton Embarcadero and Marriott near Market St). Estimated corporate rates: $280-350/night depending on room type. If ZenithAI prefers to run travel through your procurement, please share the procurement contact and PO timeline. If youd like Redwood to book on your behalf, we can accept a PO or a charge-back arrangement.

Procurement: Please loop in procurement@zenithai.com (and any internal PO number). Our commercial team will prepare a simple SOW + invoice to attach to PO. Deal reference: Z-2027-0843.

AV & streaming: Ben (ccd) is our AV lead and will share an AV matrix. We support live stream + recording; recommended vendor for larger rooms is StudioCables (local).

Visa letters/security: We can provide formal invitation/attendance letters for visa. Let us know full legal names and passport expiry dates for the two APAC travelers. No additional COVID checks required at this time.

Onsite host & logistics: Marissa will be the onsite lead for venue coordination and catering. Shell share a travel manifest template and hotel block deadlines.

Attached: draft agenda (v1) for your review. Next steps:
1) Confirm preferred week (Aug 10-12 or Aug 17-19)
2) Share procurement contact/PO timing and any billing address requirements
3) Provide visa names ASAP if needed

Thanks — Ill target a short call Wed afternoon if that works for you to finalize.

Warmly,
Aisha Rahman
Director, Solutions - Redwood Inference
[Attachment: draft_agenda_v1.pdf (PDF, 78KB)]

title: One-time token top-up and invoice-offset routing for Vertice pilot
preview:
From: Susan Park <susan@verticeinc.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, 30 May 2027 16:14:00 -0700
Subject: Request: temporary trial top-up & short extension for Vertice pilot

Hi Ben,

Hope you are well. Quick ask from our pilot team: onboarding delays used up the initial allocation faster than expected. The pilot is valuable but stalled without more tokens. Could Redwood grant a one-time top-up of ~50,000 token-equivalent credits and extend the pilot window by 30 days?

If easier for your finance team, we are also fine with you posting an invoice offset / credit memo against the next bill instead of a direct grant. Let me know what documentation you need (PO, invoice ref, DocuSign). We want to remain on track to make a purchase decision after the extension.

Thanks,
Susan
VP Product, Vertice Inc.


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 31 May 2027 09:22:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Request: temporary trial top-up & short extension for Vertice pilot

Forwarding Vertice's request. They asked for 50k token top-up + 30 day extension. They said a credit memo or direct grant both work; they can sign an amendment if needed.

Questions for Finance/Accounting:
- Preferred accounting treatment? (credit memo / invoice offset vs promotional expense)
- Is there a dollar amount threshold that requires additional approvals?
- What operational steps do we need so AR can post this cleanly?

Attached the original ask from Vertice.

Ben
Account Executive


From: Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 31 May 2027 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Request: temporary trial top-up & short extension for Vertice pilot

Ben,

Finance guidance below. Short answer: prefer a customer-visible credit memo applied to the next invoice (invoice-offset) where possible. This maintains AR controls and avoids unusual refund flows. Key points:
- If the pilot never generated an invoice, a pro forma credit applied to their first chargeable invoice is fine; record as contra-revenue.
- If any revenue was already recognized for this account, we need to reverse appropriately. Please confirm if any paid invoices exist for Vertice.
- For promotional grants (marketing expense) we have a Promotional Allowance GL; keep individual grants under $25k unless exec approves. Grants above $10k require Sales Director approval.

Operational asks: provide Deal ID, approximate USD amount for 50k tokens (we will convert), and any invoice number to offset. We'll prepare a credit memo template and a proposed journal entry for AR.

Attaching credit_memo_template.xlsx and journal_entry_example.csv.

Naomi
Director, Finance


From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 31 May 2027 13:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Request: temporary trial top-up & short extension for Vertice pilot

Operational note from Accounting: prefer a pro forma credit memo applied to next invoice for transparency in AR. If Sales wants this entirely off-books as a marketing expense, we need a one-line written business justification and Sales Director approval. Suggested approval language included below for Ben to collect.

Suggested approval drop-in:
"I approve a one-time customer credit of USD $X for Vertice Inc. (DEAL-7890) to support pilot completion due to onboarding delays. This will be recorded as an invoice offset applied to the next invoice per Redwood AR process."

Marissa
Accounting Manager

title: Borealis discovery: throughput/latency needs & next steps
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Oliver Grant <oliver@borealis.ai>, Leah Kim <leah.kim@borealis.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 30 May 2027 15:22:00 -0700
Subject: Borealis discovery: throughput/latency needs & next steps

Hi Oliver / Leah,

Thanks again for taking the demo today — really appreciated the candid discussion. Quick recap from my side so we’re aligned and can move to a technical deep-dive next week.

Top pain points you described:
- Peak inference spikes during end-of-day batch scoring (up to ~12k reqs/min).
- Tight 95th percentile latency target for customer-facing features: 150–200ms across EU region.
- Data governance: request-level audit logs and retention controls for PII-sensitive pipelines.

Goals you called out:
- Replace the current in-house host with a predictable, cost-controlled runtime to avoid build/ops overhead.
- Support both hosted (for prototyping) and a private VPC deployment for production regulated workloads.
- Keep model parity (we’ll serve a finetuned Llama variant) while improving unit economics.

Proposed next steps:
1) Technical deep-dive call (60m) with your infra lead + our serving engineers — to cover topology, batching, KV cache, and SLO tradeoffs.
2) Share a short agenda and topology sketch ahead of the call.
3) If we agree on capacity/targets, we’ll run a short POC (2–4 weeks) focused on latency percentiles and concurrency.

Tentative times — please pick any that work for you:
- Tue Jun 1, 09:00–10:00 BST
- Tue Jun 1, 14:00–15:00 BST
- Wed Jun 2, 09:00–10:00 BST

I’ve attached a 1-page agenda and a rough topology sketch that Marissa will use as the basis for the deep-dive.

Thanks,
Ben

--
Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Manager | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0192

Attachments: borealis-deepdive-agenda.pdf (agenda, 1pg), topology-sketch.png (PNG, 800x600)


From: Oliver Grant <oliver@borealis.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Leah Kim <leah.kim@borealis.ai>
Date: Mon, 31 May 2027 08:14:00 +0100
Subject: Re: Borealis discovery: throughput/latency needs & next steps

Ben — thanks for the summary and the materials, very helpful. A few clarifications from our side: 

- The 12k reqs/min figure is correct for peak scoring, but most of the day is steady at ~400–600 rps. Spiky batch jobs (internal) cause the tail behavior.
- The 150–200ms SLO is our target for the web-facing latency (95th). We can tolerate slightly higher internal latency for batch jobs.
- Audit logs: we need per-request tracing for 90 days and the ability to export logs to our Splunk instance. Can Redwood support log export + redaction controls?

On deployment model: we prefer Private (VPC) for production. Hosted for evaluation is fine, but production must be in our cloud account. Do you support dedicated reserved capacity inside a customer VPC?

Julian (our infra lead) will join the call — I’ll loop him if you need explicit infra-level questions.

We can do Tue Jun 1 at 14:00 BST. Please send calendar invite.

—Oliver
Oliver Grant
Head of Product | Borealis AI
oliver@borealis.ai | +44 20 7946 0743


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Oliver Grant <oliver@borealis.ai>, Leah Kim <leah.kim@borealis.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Julian Park <julian.park@borealis.ai>
Date: Tue, 01 Jun 2027 09:35:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Borealis discovery: throughput/latency needs & next steps

Great — thanks for the clarifications, Oliver. Confirming for Tue Jun 1, 14:00 BST (I’ve sent a calendar invite; please accept).

Short answers to your questions so we’re not blind on the call:
- Log export: yes — Redwood can stream request/response-level traces and structured audit events to external sinks (Splunk, S3). We also provide configurable redaction rules for PII fields.
- Private deployment: yes — our Private option supports customer-managed VPCs with a control plane in our region and the runtime installed into the customer account. We can also provision Dedicated capacity inside that VPC to guarantee throughput.

What we’ll cover on the technical deep-dive (agenda attached):
1) Expected traffic profile (steady vs spike) and shaping strategies (burst pools, autoscale).
2) Latency SLOs: options to hit 95th=150–200ms via batching, KV cache, and model quantization.
3) Topology options: single-region private runtime vs split control plane + regional serving for lower cross-region latency.
4) Observability & logs (retention/export/redaction).
5) POC proposal and metrics to validate.

Pre-call ask:
- If your infra team can share a sample request profile (qps over 24h, typical sequence lengths, model size), Marissa will pre-run some capacity estimates so we can discuss concrete machine counts and cost tradeoffs on the call.

Marissa (cc’d) — can you confirm you’ll cover batching, KV caching, and the quantization tradeoffs? I suggested focusing on 95th targets and the expected concurrency for the POC.

Thanks — looking forward to the call.
Ben

--
Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Manager | Redwood Inference

Attachments: borealis-deepdive-agenda.pdf (updated 1pg)


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Oliver Grant <oliver@borealis.ai>, Leah Kim <leah.kim@borealis.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Julian Park <julian.park@borealis.ai>
Date: Tue, 01 Jun 2027 11:00:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Borealis discovery: throughput/latency needs & next steps

Hi team — Marissa here, technical lead for serving at Redwood. I’ll run the deep-dive and bring concrete capacity estimates. A few notes I’ll prepare for the call:

- We'll model two scenarios: (A) single-region Private runtime with Dedicated pools sized for 12k rpm peaks and (B) hybrid split (control plane managed) with regional edge serving to reduce cross-region hops.
- Quantization: if you can confirm the model variant and enum of sequence lengths we’ll evaluate int8 vs int4 tradeoffs; often int8 gets you latency wins with minimal quality loss.
- Observability: demonstration of log export to Splunk and an example of redaction rules.

Also attaching a quick topology sketch (PNG) to illustrate the two options — we’ll iterate it on the call.

If there are any specific compliance constraints (e.g., data residency for log exports), please flag them before the meeting so we can bring the right folks.

Cheers,
Marissa
Principal Systems Engineer | Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.com

Attachments: topology-sketch.png (architecture sketch)

title: Transition plan & security gate cadence for MAP (SOC2 / SIG / DPA)
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Asha Rao <asha.rao@ledgercorp.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 1 Jun 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Transition plan & security gate cadence for MAP (SOC2 / SIG / DPA)

Asha —

Thanks again for the call this morning. I wanted to put a concrete MAP-style timeline and security gate cadence on your radar so we can align expectations with procurement and engineering. High-level goals: pilot Redwood hosted inference in staging by late June, complete security intake and contract underwriter checks in parallel, and have a signed DPA before production.

Proposed security/compliance checkpoints (draft):
- SIG/CAIQ: Ledger completes questionnaire (we'll provide the spreadsheet) — target Jun 8
- SOC 2: Redwood to provide Type II PDF + 1‑pager control scope (we'll deliver Jun 4)

preview:
From: Elena Torres <procurement@novasystems.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>
Cc: James Li <james.li@novasystems.com>
Date: Sat, 29 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: SIG questionnaire submission + two follow-ups

Hi Aisha,

Thanks again for taking our SIG request — we uploaded the completed questionnaire (attached) but have a couple of open items before we can present to our InfoSec committee next week. Two quick asks: 1) can you confirm where the key rotation procedures and evidence live for customer encryption keys (we're particularly interested in HSM usage and rotation cadence), and 2) please confirm data residency / regional isolation options for Dedicated deployments (we need an EU-only option for a subset of traffic).

I've attached our completed SIG_Questionnaire_NovaSys.xlsx and the Vendor_Risk_Form (for procurement). If helpful, James and I are available Wednesday morning for a 30-minute clarifying call.

Appreciate the quick turnaround — our timeline is tight.

Best,
Elena Torres
Senior Procurement Manager
Nova Systems
procurement@novasystems.com

Attachments: SIG_Questionnaire_NovaSys.xlsx (spreadsheet), Vendor_Risk_Form.pdf (pdf)

To: Elena Torres <procurement@novasystems.com>

Subject: Re: SIG questionnaire submission + two follow-ups

Hi Elena — received, thanks.

Quick ack: we reviewed the uploaded questionnaire and noted the two items you called out. I've looped in Karthik (security & infrastructure) for the HSM/key rotation specifics and Ben for evidence packaging (SOC 2 artefacts, diagrams).

Proposed mutual action plan:
- By EOD Monday (05/31): we will send a control mapping spreadsheet with line-by-line answers populated (I attached our SIG_Control_Mapping_Template.xlsx to this thread).
- By Wednesday (06/02): deliver the requested artefacts (HSM architecture diagram, rotation policy excerpt, and a short statement on regional isolation options for Dedicated).
- If any items need legal/redline review (procurement form), Ben will coordinate with Legal and get back to you by Thursday.

Would you like a 30-minute call Wednesday morning to review the mapping and any exceptions? If so, propose 10:00–10:30 PT.

Thanks — Aisha
Aisha Rahman
Head of Sales, Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference
aisha_rahman@redwood.com

Attachments: SIG_Control_Mapping_Template.xlsx (xlsx)


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>, Elena Torres <procurement@novasystems.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 31 May 2027 08:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: SIG questionnaire submission + two follow-ups

Elena / Aisha — following up with the technical mapping and answers to the two items you highlighted. I populated the template with concise responses; below are the key excerpts and where to find evidence. Full mapping is in the shared Drive link.

1) Customer key management / HSM usage
- Control summary: Customer data at rest is encrypted using customer-managed keys (CKMS) or Redwood-managed keys depending on plan. For Dedicated/private deployments we support KMS integrations and HSM-backed key storage.
- Rotation policy: Root/intermediate key rotation cadence: annual; Data-encryption key (DEK) rotation: in-place on rekey or compromise; automated key versioning via our KMS connectors.
- Evidence: HSM-arch-diagram.pdf (architecture diagram), rotation-policy-excerpt.txt (policy excerpt), and key-rotation audit sample (redacted) are in the Drive folder: https://drive.redwood.com/shared/NOVA-SIG-2027/artifacts/key-management

2) Regional isolation (Dedicated)
- We support region-specific Dedicated pools (EU-only, US-only). Networking: VPC peering or PrivateLink options; egress controls can be limited to a single cloud region.
- Evidence: deployment-topology-eu.png and our Dedicated SLA excerpt. See Drive: https://drive.redwood.com/shared/NOVA-SIG-2027/artifacts/deployment

Notes and exceptions:
- For Hosted (shared) customers, token caching within the runtime may persist KV prefixes for short periods to optimize latency; caching TTLs are configurable and we can provide a policy statement.
- We need to confirm the legal/contractual language Nova requires for customer-managed KMS—Ben, can you pick this up with Procurement?

Happy to jump on the Wednesday call if you want a short walkthrough of the key-management folder.

-Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Director, Infrastructure Security
karthik_iyer@redwood.com


From: Elena Torres <procurement@novasystems.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>
Cc: James Li <james.li@novasystems.com>
Date: Mon, 31 May 2027 11:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: SIG questionnaire submission + two follow-ups

Karthik — this is helpful, thanks. A couple of very specific follow-ups before our InfoSec review:

- Can you provide an excerpt showing the HSM vendor (Yubico/Thales/CloudHSM) and whether keys are tenant-separated vs multi-tenant within the HSM cluster? Our auditors require tenant separation for customer-managed keys.
- For the KV cache TTL you mentioned: please provide the default TTL value, how it's configured per-customer, and a short statement we can include in the risk form that says whether cached prefixes contain plaintext or only encrypted tokens.

I've attached our Vendor_Risk_Form.pdf with the relevant sections highlighted. If you can drop the two items into the Drive folder by COB today, that would keep us on schedule.

Also, we confirmed internally that Nova will require EU-only Dedicated routing for a pilot set of traffic — can you confirm a test timeline for spinning up an EU-only pool? We expect to start pilot traffic week of 6/14, so needs to be available by 6/10.

Thanks — Elena

Attachments: Vendor_Risk_Form.pdf (pdf)


From: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>
To: Elena Torres <procurement@novasystems.com>, James Li <james.li@novasystems.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>

Action items we committed to (recap):
- Karthik: upload HSM vendor & tenancy statement + a short TTL/caching statement to Drive (target: COB today) — artifacts will be in https://drive.redwood.com/shared/NOVA-SIG-2027/artifacts/key-management
- Ben: review procurement/Vendor_Risk_Form and propose contract language for customer-managed KMS (target: 6/3)
- Monica (Ops): confirm EU-only Dedicated pool spin-up timeline and test readiness (target: 6/10); she will also share a brief checklist for network peering and egress controls.
- Aisha: schedule the Wednesday 10:00 PT call and send calendar invite (invite sent).

If anything slips, we'll flag and propose alternate dates. Otherwise, we'll plan to surface the assembled evidence and a 15–20 minute walkthrough during Wednesday's call.

Thanks all — look forward to wrapping this up quickly.

title: Integration kickoff — SDK/auth/routing + request/response schema questions
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Samir Patel <samir@vertex-analytics.com>, Anna Lopez <anna.lopez@cloudport.io>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-05-30T14:02:00-07:00
Subject: Integration kickoff — SDK/auth/routing + request/response schema questions

Hi Samir / Anna — thanks again for the demo today. Quick recap and a handful of concrete questions so we can scope the prototype: 

1) Auth: you mentioned org-wide identity and rotating credentials. For the prototype, do you prefer: (a) OAuth2 client_credentials + short-lived tokens managed by your ops, (b) mTLS between Redwood Private + your VPC, or (c) long-lived API keys behind our Credential Proxy? We need to map this to our SDK onboarding flow (we support all three but want to minimize friction).

2) Routing & regions: You said traffic should prefer us-east-1, with failover to us-west for DR. Can you confirm expected RPS and burst characteristics (typical vs peak)? That will inform our pooling / dedicated capacity plan.

3) Request/response schema: Important for your use-case is deterministic structured outputs (tool-calling + JSON record). Two quick asks: do you need strict schema validation server-side, or can the client accept a best-effort JSON with a validation step? Also, are there required fields that must be present for downstream ingestion?

4) Embeddings & rerankers: You described a semantic-retrieval + rerank pipeline. Are you planning to store embeddings in your vector DB and call our reranker, or would you rather we return k candidates and run reranking client-side?

5) Chat vs completions / tokenization: A couple details we want to lock down — will you be using a chat-style session (canonical role/message history) or single-turn completions? Related: what max response length expectations do you have (max_tokens) and do you rely on stop sequences for termination? Known edge cases: tokenization of non-ASCII payloads and newlines can shift token counts — if you have hard limits we should test early.

6) Streaming semantics: If you want low-latency partial outputs, we can enable streaming (delta chunks + final object). If you also depend on strict JSON schema, streaming needs to support incremental deltas with an eventual full validation pass (we have patterns for that, but want to check acceptance).

Attachments: Redwood SDK onboarding checklist (redwood_sdk_onboarding_checklist.pdf) attached — includes example auth flows and a proposal for default SDK config.

Proposed next steps:
- Samir: confirm auth preference and RPS ranges
- Anna: confirm whether client-side rerank is required
- Redwood (Marissa + Laura): produce a 2-week prototype plan and a minimal schema proposal for the first integration

Thanks — Ben

--
Ben Carter
Senior Sales Engineer | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai


From: Samir Patel <samir@vertex-analytics.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Anna Lopez <anna.lopez@cloudport.io>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-05-30T15:10:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Integration kickoff — SDK/auth/routing + request/response schema questions

Ben — great notes, appreciate the thoroughness. Short answers inline: 

1) Auth: we prefer OAuth2 client_credentials with a short TTL managed by our identity team. We'll supply client_id/client_secret and rotate on a 24h cadence. We can stand up a sidecar to refresh tokens if that helps. mTLS is possible later but not for the POC.

2) Routing & regions: typical is 15 RPS with spikes to ~120 RPS during batch jobs. Bursts are short (minutes). Primary region us-east, fallback us-west acceptable.

3) Schema: we need server-side validation for required ingestion fields (id, timestamp, classification_label). If Redwood can return a structured JSON envelope and a validation result flag that would be ideal.

4) Embeddings & rerank: we'll host vectors in Pinecone; for the POC we'd like Redwood to return top-k candidates and our service will do the rerank client-side (cosine similarities + business heuristics). If later we want Redwood to do rerank, we'd evaluate SLA and cost.

5) Chat vs completions / tokenization: we'll use chat sessions (conversation state matters). For max_tokens: target generation limit ~512 tokens; we also have an overall protocol message size cap at 8k tokens. We do rely on stop sequences sometimes (e.g., "
---END---
") for deterministic cutoffs. Note our data includes multi-lingual user inputs — please add tokenization tests for UTF-8 sequences and Chinese/Japanese samples.

6) Streaming: streaming is desirable for the UX. We expect partial deltas (streaming tokens) and a final full JSON payload for validation. If streaming could optionally include incremental "partial_json" fields that the client can use to render progressive UI without depending on final validation, that would be great.

Attaching a short note from our infra team with networking constraints (vertex_security_vpn_details.docx).

Thanks — Samir
Principal Product Manager | Vertex Analytics
samir@vertex-analytics.com


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-05-30T16:03:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: Integration kickoff — SDK/auth/routing + request/response schema questions

Forwarding Samir's answers — key takeaways for the engineering plan.

On 2027-05-30 15:10, Samir Patel <samir@vertex-analytics.com> wrote:
> Ben — great notes, appreciate the thoroughness. Short answers inline: 
> 
> 1) Auth: we prefer OAuth2 client_credentials with a short TTL managed by our identity team...
> 2) Routing & regions: typical is 15 RPS with spikes to ~120 RPS...
> 3) Schema: we need server-side validation for required ingestion fields (id, timestamp, classification_label).
> 4) Embeddings & rerank: we'll host vectors in Pinecone; want top-k from Redwood, rerank client-side.
> 5) Chat vs completions / tokenization: chat sessions, generation limit ~512, overall cap 8k tokens, use stop sequences ("---END---").
> 6) Streaming: want partial deltas + final validated JSON; optional partial_json in stream would be helpful.

Suggested immediate tasks: 
- Marissa: draft auth flow for OAuth2 client_credentials with refresh pattern for SDK (include sample curl and SDK snippet).
- Karthik: define streaming contract for chat sessions with delta events + final envelope; include how we surface validation errors to the client.
- Laura: produce minimal schema (id, timestamp, classification_label + optional metadata) and a validation strategy (soft vs hard reject).

I think we can scope a 2-week POC: week 1 — auth + routing + sample call (non-streaming) + schema validation; week 2 — streaming + embedding handoff + client-side rerank example.

Ben


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Cc: Samir Patel <samir@vertex-analytics.com>
Date: 2027-05-30T18:27:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Integration kickoff — SDK/auth/routing + request/response schema questions

Thanks — looping in proposed technical details so we can assign tasks.

Auth (Marissa): we'll implement OAuth2 client_credentials in the SDK with auto-refresh. Proposal: SDK holds a refresh worker that fetches client credentials from a config store, requests token from your token endpoint, and emits tokens to the request pipeline. We'll include sample code and an env var to toggle between client_credentials and api_key for tests.

Streaming contract (Karthik): propose event stream envelope with types: 
- event.type = "delta" (partial tokens/strings), payload = "delta_text"
- event.type = "partial_json" (best-effort JSON fragment)
- event.type = "final" (complete structured JSON + validation_status)

Notes on tokenization and max_tokens (Laura): important distinction — our max_tokens param will apply to generated tokens only (i.e., not count prompt tokens in the same parameter). We'll document recommended buffer sizes for UTF-8 heavy payloads. For stop sequences we'll support both string and regex matches; for streaming we will emit a final event once stop is observed server-side. Edge-case: when a stop sequence splits across chunks we must buffer until we can confirm — we'll include tests.

Schema validation: draft YAML attached (schema_proposal.yaml). For the POC we suggest hard validation only for required ingestion fields (id, timestamp, classification_label); for other fields we'll return a validation_warnings array. Client-side rerank example: we'll add a sample that requests top-10 candidates from Redwood, then runs cosine similarity and a simple business filter.

I'll assign the write-ups and aim to have a POC checklist + runbook by EOD Friday. Samir — any constraints on SLA/throughput for the POC aside from the RPS numbers? Also, can you share a small sample dataset (5-10 multilingual queries) for tokenization tests?

—Marissa
Principal Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.ai
text:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Samir Patel <samir@vertex-analytics.com>, Anna Lopez <anna.lopez@cloudport.io>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-05-30T14:02:00-07:00
Subject: Integration kickoff — SDK/auth/routing + request/response schema questions

Hi Samir / Anna — thanks again for the demo today. Quick recap and a handful of concrete questions so we can scope the prototype: 

1) Auth: you mentioned org-wide identity and rotating credentials. For the prototype, do you prefer: (a) OAuth2 client_credentials + short-lived tokens managed by your ops, (b) mTLS between Redwood Private + your VPC, or (c) long-lived API keys behind our Credential Proxy? We need to map this to our SDK onboarding flow (we support all three but want to minimize friction).

2) Routing & regions: You said traffic should prefer us-east-1, with failover to us-west for DR. Can you confirm expected RPS and burst characteristics (typical vs peak)? That will inform our pooling / dedicated capacity plan.

3) Request/response schema: Important for your use-case is deterministic structured outputs (tool-calling + JSON record). Two quick asks: do you need strict schema validation server-side, or can the client accept a best-effort JSON with a validation step? Also, are there required fields that must be present for downstream ingestion?

4) Embeddings & rerankers: You described a semantic-retrieval + rerank pipeline. Are you planning to store embeddings in your vector DB and call our reranker, or would you rather we return k candidates and run reranking client-side?

5) Chat vs completions / tokenization: A couple details we want to lock down — will you be using a chat-style session (canonical role/message history) or single-turn completions? Related: what max response length expectations do you have (max_tokens) and do you rely on stop sequences for termination? Known edge cases: tokenization of non-ASCII payloads and newlines can shift token counts — if you have hard limits we should test early.

6) Streaming semantics: If you want low-latency partial outputs, we can enable streaming (delta chunks + final object). If you also depend on strict JSON schema, streaming needs to support incremental deltas with an eventual full validation pass (we have patterns for that, but want to check acceptance).

Attachments: Redwood SDK onboarding checklist (redwood_sdk_onboarding_checklist.pdf) attached — includes example auth flows and a proposal for default SDK config.

Proposed next steps:
- Samir: confirm auth preference and RPS ranges
- Anna: confirm whether client-side rerank is required
- Redwood (Marissa + Laura): produce a 2-week prototype plan and a minimal schema proposal for the first integration

Thanks — Ben

--
Ben Carter
Senior Sales Engineer | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai


From: Samir Patel <samir@vertex-analytics.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Anna Lopez <anna.lopez@cloudport.io>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-05-30T15:10:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Integration kickoff — SDK/auth/routing + request/response schema questions

Ben — great notes, appreciate the thoroughness. Short answers inline: 

1) Auth: we prefer OAuth2 client_credentials with a short TTL managed by our identity team. We'll supply client_id/client_secret and rotate on a 24h cadence. We can stand up a sidecar to refresh tokens if that helps. mTLS is possible later but not for the POC.

2) Routing & regions: typical is 15 RPS with spikes to ~120 RPS during batch jobs. Bursts are short (minutes). Primary region us-east, fallback us-west acceptable.

3) Schema: we need server-side validation for required ingestion fields (id, timestamp, classification_label). If Redwood can return a structured JSON envelope and a validation result flag that would be ideal.

4) Embeddings & rerank: we'll host vectors in Pinecone; for the POC we'd like Redwood to return top-k candidates and our service will do the rerank client-side (cosine similarities + business heuristics). If later we want Redwood to do rerank, we'd evaluate SLA and cost.

5) Chat vs completions / tokenization: we'll use chat sessions (conversation state matters). For max_tokens: target generation limit ~512 tokens; we also have an overall protocol message size cap at 8k tokens. We do rely on stop sequences sometimes (e.g., "
---END---
") for deterministic cutoffs. Note our data includes multi-lingual user inputs — please add tokenization tests for UTF-8 sequences and Chinese/Japanese samples.

6) Streaming: streaming is desirable for the UX. We expect partial deltas (streaming tokens) and a final full JSON payload for validation. If streaming could optionally include incremental "partial_json" fields that the client can use to render progressive UI without depending on final validation, that would be great.

Attaching a short note from our infra team with networking constraints (vertex_security_vpn_details.docx).

Thanks — Samir
Principal Product Manager | Vertex Analytics
samir@vertex-analytics.com

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 28 May 2027 15:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Altum renewal — initial orderform and scope for term change + capacity uplift

Ben / Lauren —

I've reviewed Altum's tracked changes and proposed the following consolidated counter (high level):

1) Term / Termination: move to a 12-month fixed term with a 60-day termination-for-convenience right (compromise between Altum's 30/45-day ask and our preference). If they exercise termination for convenience within the first 6 months, apply a 50% remaining term cancellation fee (commercial mitigation). After 6 months, provide pro-rata refund for unused prepaid amounts.

2) Data retention: accept a narrow operational logs retention clause limited to system telemetry and request a defined data deletion SLA (30 days) for customer-requested deletions. Carve out regulated data for customer-managed retention controls.

3) Pricing clarity: accept uplift as a committed capacity line item on the order form (annualized). Overage billed monthly at the published overage rate (proposed rate included in my markup).

4) SSO/SAML: we cannot commit to a full enterprise SSO rollout by 2027-07-15. Propose a phased delivery: 2027-07-15 for initial SAML test integration (dev environment) and full production SSO by 2027-09-01. Include rollback/fallback support in case integration reveals blocking issues.

5) SOC2 evidence: we can provide SOC2 Type II report within 30 days of a formal evidence request; operational playbook to be provided as a separate attachment.

I've attached my annotated redline (karthik_annotated_redline.docx) with suggested language. Once we all align on the commercial terms I can produce a clean counter (Redwood_Counter_v1.docx) for signature.

Next steps: Ben, if you can confirm budget owner and billing contact I will finalize language. Lauren, please confirm we are okay with the cancellation fee structure.

Regards,

title: MSA & Order Form redlines — sync on staggered notice offsets and TfC sequencing
preview:
From: Jordan Ellis <j.ellis@beaconhealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurement@beaconhealth.com
Date: Tue, 25 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Beacon MSA redlines (term/renewal/exit)

Hi Ben —

Attached are Beacon's redlines to the MSA and the draft order form. The three items our counsel flagged are:

1) Auto-renew length and unilateral renewal language (section 2.1) — they want a 30-day notice window, not 60.
2) Termination for convenience (TfC) — propose a 90-day notice plus a pro-rata refund for unused prepaid committed capacity.
3) Notice offsets across order forms — they want a single, top-level opt-out to control region-specific pilots.

Can your team confirm whether Redwood can accept a 30-day opt-out and the proposed TfC payment mechanics? Also loop in legal if you need to discuss alternate language we can accept.

Attachments: Beacon_MSA_Redlines_v3.pdf, OrderForm_v2_redlined.docx

Thanks,
Jordan Ellis
Senior Counsel, Beacon Health

-----Original Message-----
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
To: Jordan Ellis <j.ellis@beaconhealth.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 25 May 2027 12:37:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Beacon MSA redlines (term/renewal/exit)

Jordan — thanks for sending this through and for the clear summary.

Quick internal plan: I want legal to take the lead on the TfC language and the refund mechanics; commercial will weigh in on the notice window and staggered offsets across pilots. Laura/Karthik — can you pick this up?

From a commercial perspective: 30-day notice is aggressive for larger committed pools — we typically ask for 60 to allow capacity reallocation. That said, for pilots and early-stage customers we can be more flexible if we build in staged notice offsets and a defined rebate formula.

Next steps I propose:
- Laura: legal redline suggestion on TfC payment mechanics and whether pro-rata + notice termination is acceptable or needs a capped fee.
- Karthik: commercial note with proposed staggered notice offsets (pilot vs production) and suggested language for order form exhibit.
- I will schedule a 30-min sync with Jordan and Beacon procurement to walk through tradeoffs — is Thursday 5/27 at 9am PT OK?

Ben
Head of Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference

-----Original Message-----
From: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 26 May 2027 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Beacon MSA redlines (term/renewal/exit)

Ben/Karthik — attaching a short amendment and suggested language for TfC (file: Redwood_TfC_Amendment_suggestion.docx). Key points from legal review: 

- We can accept a 30-day opt-out for customers with annual commitments under $250k and for pilot/order-form-limited commitments, provided we insert an explicit transition assistance clause and a 30-day capacity reallocation buffer (to avoid sudden memory on GPU pools).

- For TfC payment mechanics: recommend compromise language — pro-rata refund of prepayments for unused time, less a 10% administrative processing fee capped at $50k. This balances Beacon's ask and Redwood's need to cover committed reservation costs.

- Regarding single top-level opt-out across multiple order forms: suggest clarifying that an order form can be designated as "pilot" at execution, which triggers the reduced notice window and different refund mechanics. That avoids global overrides that impact production order forms.

I also flagged the auto-renew clause: we should keep 60-day default for production order forms and allow 30-day for pilot-designated order forms. That is acceptable internally.

Questions for the sync:
- Would Beacon accept the administrative fee/cap?
- Can they mark which order forms will be pilot vs production at signing?

Laura Bennett
Legal Ops, Redwood Inference

Attachment: Redwood_TfC_Amendment_suggestion.docx

-----Original Message-----
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jordan Ellis <j.ellis@beaconhealth.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 27 May 2027 15:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Beacon MSA redlines (term/renewal/exit)

Looping Jordan in with a commercial view and proposed sequencing. Jordan — thanks for the openness. Two commercial constraints to be aware of:

1) Capacity reallocation: for committed Dedicated pools we need a 60-day lead to spin down reserved capacity and reassign GPUs without impacting other customers.
2) Billing reconciliation: pro-rata refunds are straightforward for monthly subscriptions; for prepaid annual commitments there's an operational lag (up to 45 days) to reconcile usage and issue credits.

Proposed practical compromise (to include in the order form exhibit):
- Pilot order forms: 30-day notice; pro-rata refund for unused prepaid time with a 10% admin fee (cap $50k).
- Production order forms: 60-day notice; no pro-rata refund for reserved committed capacity (instead a termination fee equal to 25% of remaining committed value) OR allow Sunny (rev ops) to approve pro-rata on a case-by-case basis.
- Staggered notice offsets: allow the customer to elect pilot status per order form at signature, and include an explicit exhibit that defines pilot vs production and the exact refund/fee math.

If Beacon agrees to pilot designation controls and the admin fee cap, we can accept the 30-day ask for pilots. Otherwise, we need to hold firm at 60 for production.

Action items:
- Ben: confirm sync time.
- Jordan: confirm whether Beacon can toggle pilot flags on order forms at execution and whether procurement will accept a capped processing fee.

Thanks — Karthik
VP, Sales Engineering

-----Original Message-----
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
To: Jordan Ellis <j.ellis@beaconhealth.com>, procurement@beaconhealth.com
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 29 May 2027 16:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Beacon MSA redlines (term/renewal/exit)

Jordan / Beacon team — thanks for the productive thread and for surfacing the redlines. Short summary and agreement path from Redwood: 

1) Pilot vs Production: Beacon will mark order forms as "pilot" at signature to trigger the 30-day opt-out and pilot refund mechanics. Production order forms will default to 60-day notice.

2) TfC payment mechanics: Redwood will accept pro-rata refunds for pilot order forms less a 10% administrative processing fee, capped at $50k. For production committed pools, Redwood will either apply a 25% termination charge or pursue case-by-case reconciliation via rev ops.

3) Notice offsets & exhibit: Redwood will add an exhibit to the order form that defines pilot designation, the notice windows, and the exact refund/fee calculation (we will circulate a version after this thread).

4) Legal asks: Laura to circulate the proposed amendment language (attached) and we will run a short review with Beacon counsel.

Can we lock a 30-min call on Monday 5/31 at 10am PT to finalize language? If that works, I'll send a calendar invite and a draft exhibit for review beforehand.

Ben
Head of Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference

Attachments referenced: Beacon_MSA_Redlines_v3.pdf (Beacon), OrderForm_v2_redlined.docx (Beacon), Redwood_TfC_Amendment_suggestion.docx (Redwood)
