From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Erin Shaw <erin.shaw@stellarhealth.com>, Procurement Team <procurement@stellarhealth.com>
Date: Wed, 26 May 2027 10:15:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Next steps: vendor enrollment & tax docs after demo with StellarHealth

Hi all — quick finance confirmation: 

- The W-9 we provided (Redwood_W9_2027.pdf) is sufficient for US tax ID verification. For bank wiring/ACH we need the completed banking section of the vendor packet (account name, routing, account number) and a bank letter or voided check would expedite validation.
- We do not strictly require a signed MSA before a PO is issued; many customers issue a PO referencing agreed commercial terms and finalize the MSA in parallel. If Stellar needs redlines, send them over and we will prioritize review.
- For Treasury contact: please have them reach out to treasury@redwoodinference.com and reference DEAL-8342 to speed routing.

Neha — I'll approve the vendor portal invite content. Ben — happy to join any alignment call with their Treasury if needed.

Regards,
Marcus Lin
Director, Finance Operations | Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com

title: Security evidence mapping + calendar ops for SOC2/DPA review
Merchant gateway decline: evidence bundle & manual token-swap plan
preview:
From: Marta Ruiz <marta@heliontech.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 24 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Charge attempt failed for invoice #78312 — need help

Hi Avery,

We tried to process the May invoice for ACCT-HT-11 (invoice 78312) and Stripe returned a gateway decline from the acquirer. The customer's card is still active on file, but the acquirer is asking for a 3DS flow and a merchant-side pre-auth proof. Can Redwood help avoid a dunning escalation?

We attached the raw Stripe decline CSV and the merchant-facing invoice. The acquirer's message looks like: "authentication required - redirect to issuer (3DS)". They also requested evidence that the merchant attempted a pre-auth before capture.

Options from our side: 1) ask customer to re-run 3DS via hosted pay link, 2) split the invoice and post a token-swap with manual authorization, or 3) provide acquirer requested evidence and ask for manual reprocess.

We need a recommendation and if possible a short ops play (what we need from Helion + what Redwood will do). This customer is on a committed plan (DL-78312) so we want to avoid interruption.

Thanks,
Marta Ruiz
Head of Finance, Helion Technologies
attachments: stripe-decline-log-78312.csv, invoice-78312.pdf


From: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>
To: Marta Ruiz <marta@heliontech.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 24 May 2027 10:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Charge attempt failed for invoice #78312 — need help

Marta — thanks for the context and the files.

Quick summary of what I see: acquirer demanded a 3DS authentication; Stripe declined at capture. If Helion can trigger 3DS from the customer, that is the safest long-term path. Short-term we can attempt a manual token-swap + off-session authorization if Helion provides the new token (or the customer re-enters card).

Recommended immediate playbook (short):
- Helion: gather customer consent and either re-run a hosted pay link or send us a fresh card token (preferred: token). If you can ask the customer to authenticate 3DS, that's ideal.
- Redwood: if you send a new token, Payments Ops will do a manual token-swap and re-attempt capture in a probed window (we'll flag for minimal risk). If 3DS appears mandatory at auth-time we'll mark capture for manual review and add pre-auth evidence.

I'll loop in Ben from Payments Ops to coordinate the manual token-swap and provide the exact evidence bundle acquirer wants.

—Avery
Avery Johnson
Account Executive, Redwood Inference


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>, Marta Ruiz <marta@heliontech.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 24 May 2027 13:48:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Charge attempt failed for invoice #78312 — need help

Thanks for the ping. A few operational notes from Payments Ops: 

1) If Helion can produce a fresh token (stripe token or vaulted instrument id) we can do a manual token-swap in Stripe and attempt capture in a dedicated retry window (we aim for off-hours to reduce impact).
2) If acquirer requires 3DS, token-swap alone might not bypass the auth requirement — the token must be generated after successful 3DS.
3) Required evidence bundle for appeals: (a) pre-auth request logs, (b) merchant session IDs, (c) the invoice PDF, (d) the Stripe decline CSV — all of which Marta attached except the session IDs.

Action items for Helion: send (a) the new token or re-run 3DS via this hosted pay link (preferred): https://pay.heliontech.com/3ds?invoice=78312 and (b) session IDs or customer consent copy.

If Helion sends the token, please attach a short note authorizing Redwood to perform a manual token-swap & capture attempt on your behalf (one-paragraph consent). Once we get that, I'll raise a manual capture job and notify escalation if the acquirer rejects.

Attachments referenced: acquirer-screencap-3ds.png (for acquirer message), stripe-decline-log-78312.csv

-Ben
Ben Carter
Payments Operations, Redwood Inference


From: Marta Ruiz <marta@heliontech.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 25 May 2027 08:17:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Charge attempt failed for invoice #78312 — need help

Hi Ben/Avery — appreciate the quick turnaround.

We just reached the customer and they completed a 3DS flow via the hosted link. They also sent back a consent line: "I authorize Helion to use my card for invoice 78312" (included below). I attached the acquirer screencap as requested.

Consent copy: "I, Jordan Li, accept Helion Technologies charging the card used in the 3DS flow for invoice #78312."

Please proceed with token-swap and re-attempt capture. Let me know if you need anything else (session IDs — we may need our engineering team to pull those if Payments Ops requires them).

Thanks,
Marta


From: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>, Marta Ruiz <marta@heliontech.com>
Date: Tue, 25 May 2027 14:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Charge attempt failed for invoice #78312 — need help

Great — thanks for confirming, Marta. Ben: please proceed with the manual token-swap and submit the evidence bundle to the acquirer if capture fails.

Operational checklist I'm adding here for the record:
- Payments Ops: perform manual token-swap in Stripe (note: use sandbox fallback if you hit rate limits)
- If capture declines again with 3DS-required, escalate to Acquirer Liaison with attached evidence (pre-auth logs + consent + invoice) and request a re-review window of 24 hours.
- If acquirer issues a chargeback, notify AE (Avery) and CS and prepare a rebuttal packet within 7 days.

I've created a DocuSign authorization request for Helion in case we need their signed permission for manual processing: https://docusign.net/XYZ-78312

I'll monitor and will update once the swap job completes.

Laura Bennett
Director, Customer Payments & Risk

title: Elara Security: Hosted API data handoff, retention and deletion details
preview:
From: Aaron Patel <aaron.patel@elarahealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.inference>
Cc: sec-questions@elarahealth.com
Date: Sun, 23 May 2027 16:12:00 -0400
Subject: Hosted API - residency / retention / deletion specifics

Hi Ben,

Thanks for taking this — our security questionnaire has a few focused items about the Hosted API. Can you confirm the following for Elara's security review?

1) Where (region/az) is customer request payload and response data stored by default?
2) Typical retention windows for:
   - request/response bodies
   - short-term caches / KV cache
   - logs and billing records
   - backups and disaster recovery snapshots
3) Process and SLA for a customer-requested deletion / complete data handoff including backups
4) Any exceptions (legal hold, backups, analytics) and how they affect deletion timelines

If you can send a short write-up or a PDF we can attach to the questionnaire that would be great. We have an internal deadline of Friday — appreciate any urgency.

Best,
Aaron
Aaron Patel
Security Engineer, Elara Health
---
Elara Health


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.inference>
To: Aaron Patel <aaron.patel@elarahealth.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.inference>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.inference>
Date: Mon, 24 May 2027 09:08:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Hosted API - residency / retention / deletion specifics

Aaron — thanks for the clear list. Putting a concise summary below and attaching a PDF draft of answers + a short CSV retention matrix for ops. Kimberly and Marissa are cc'd for technical/legal confirmation.

High-level summary (Hosted API):
- Data residency: Customer requests are processed in the region you select at API invocation. By default we route tenancy to US (us-east-1). EU region (eu-west-1) available as opt-in. Data at rest for tenant-specific artifacts lives in the selected region; control plane metadata may be replicated to a minimal subset of regions for service health and billing.

- Retention windows (default / configurable):
  * Request & response bodies: default 14 days encrypted at rest; configurable via contract to 0 days (ephemeral) up to 90 days for debugging / analytics.
  * Short-term KV cache (in-memory / prefix cache): transient, typical TTL 0–120 minutes depending on workload; never persisted to long-term storage unless explicitly turned on by a customer config.
  * Logs & billing records: anonymized billing metrics retained 365 days; debug logs that may include metadata (not full request bodies by default) retained 90 days.
  * Backups / snapshots: disaster recovery snapshots retained 90 days by default; backup retention can be scoped per-enterprise agreement.

- Deletion & handoff process:
  1) Customer files a support portal request with account + description.
  2) We schedule a data purge workflow that removes: live storage blobs, KV caches, and removes pointers from indexes.
  3) Live data & caches: typically removed within 48–72 hours.
  4) Backups and snapshots: purged in the next backup cycle; full purging (including removal from snapshot backups) completes within 30 calendar days (standard); can be accelerated as a contractual SLA to 7–14 days for enterprise deals with appropriate audit support.
  5) We provide a deletion attestation when complete; timeline and scope will be documented per request.

- Exceptions: legal holds or court orders supersede deletion requests and will be flagged; we preserve evidence only as required by law. Analytics aggregates and billing metrics are retained in non-identifiable form and are not reversible to PII.

Compliance notes: SOC 2 Type II + KMS-backed encryption for data at rest; encryption-in-transit via TLS 1.3; audit logs available on request (per agreement).

Attachments: elara_security_answers_draft.pdf (draft language), s3-log-retention-matrix.csv (ops retention table).

Next steps: Kimberly — can you confirm the backup purge SLA wording? Marissa — can you confirm the legal-hold/legal exceptions blurb? Aaron — once we have internal sign-off I'll send the finalized PDF you can attach to the questionnaire.

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive 
Redwood Inference


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.inference>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.inference>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.inference>
Date: Mon, 24 May 2027 09:10:00 -0700
Subject: Internal: quick wording check for Elara deletion SLA

Kimberly / Marissa —

Two quick asks before I finalize the PDF:
1) Backup purge SLA — I wrote “full purging of backups completes within 30 calendar days; can be accelerated to 7–14 days for enterprise with audit support”. Is that accurate and defensible operationally?
2) Legal hold phrasing — currently: “Legal holds or lawful requests override deletion; Redwood will notify customer and preserve evidence only to the extent required.” Any necessary adjustments?

If you can reply with short edits I'll incorporate and ship to Aaron.

— Ben


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.inference>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.inference>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.inference>
Date: Tue, 25 May 2027 10:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Internal: quick wording check for Elara deletion SLA

Ben — quick answers:
1) Backup purge SLA: ops confirms 30 calendar days as the standard window for full purge from all backup tiers (we delete live data within 72 hours, then target next snapshot cycles to remove references; complete scrub from cold backups can take up to 30 days). We can offer 7–14 day accelerated purge for enterprise with an additional one-time verification fee and acceptance of a narrow audit scope; include that as an optional SLA.

2) Legal hold: tweak to be explicit about notification and scope: “Legal holds or lawful process will supersede deletion requests. Redwood will notify the customer of the legal hold and preserve only the narrowly scoped data required; Redwood will produce a scope and retention duration for the hold and will resume standard deletion workflows when the hold is lifted.”

Marissa — please flag any legal language additions; if you're good I think we can finalize.

Thanks,
Kimberly
Security & Product
Redwood Inference


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.inference>
To: Aaron Patel <aaron.patel@elarahealth.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.inference>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.inference>
Date: Tue, 25 May 2027 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Hosted API - residency / retention / deletion specifics (final)

Aaron — attaching the finalized PDF with the confirmed wording (elara_security_answers_draft.pdf). Below are the key points pulled from the document for quick reference:

- Residency: data stored in customer-selected region at API invocation (default US / us-east-1). EU and APAC regions available by contract. Control plane metadata replicated minimally for availability.
- Retention defaults: request/response bodies 14 days (configurable 0–90 days); KV cache TTL usually 0–120 minutes; debug logs 90 days; billing metrics 365 days; backups 90 days.
- Deletion workflow & SLAs: live data removed in 48–72 hours after a verified support request; backup snapshot purge completes within 30 calendar days by default. Accelerated enterprise purge (7–14 days) is available as an SLA addendum with verification support.
- Legal holds: lawful process supersedes deletion; Redwood will notify customer, preserve only scoped data, and publish hold scope and duration; deletion resumes after release of hold.

Attachments: elara_security_answers_draft.pdf (finalized answers) — please confirm receipt and let us know if you need a redline or a signed attestation.

Best regards,

Subject: Discovery recap — two quick questions and a proposed next step

Jordan — great to meet earlier. Quick recap from our 30-min call and two short questions so we can prioritize next steps on our side: 

1) What outcome matters most for Roaster Labs in a 6–8 week pilot? (reduce inference cost, lower latency for checkout, or something else?)
2) Is procurement aligned to a short pilot (PO within 30 days) or would you need a longer approval window?

High-level notes:
- Integrations: you have a microservices stack (Go) + AWS Fargate for API hosting; team prefers a VPC/private deployment for data controls.
- Usage profile: 200–600 requests/minute with 80% short (<128 tokens) completions and a small set of batch embedding jobs overnight.
- Success metrics we discussed: p95 latency <= 150ms, 30–40% cost reduction vs current setup, and clear audit logs for RPCs.

Attached: Discovery_Summary_BenCarter_v1.pdf — a 1-page recap and proposed pilot approach (scope, success metrics, estimated infra profile). Also included a SOW template in case procurement wants a starting draft.

Proposed next step: if those two answers look right, I can: 1) send a simple pilot SOW for your legal/procurement, and 2) get Monica (Solutions Engineer) to scope a short technical runbook for on-prem/VPC connectivity. Can you tell me which outcome you’d prioritize and the procurement timeline?

Thanks,
Ben Carter

ben@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0124

From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jared Lin <jared@cloudco.com>
Cc: Priya Sharma <priya.sharma@cloudco.com>, Kevin Ortiz <kevin.ortiz@procure.cloudco.com>, Mina Haddad <mina_haddad@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 21 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: SIG / CAIQ annual refresh — request to escrow critical artifacts in Vendr

Hi Jared / Priya —

Following up on last quarter's intake: CloudCo is in scope for our annual SIG / CAIQ refresh and SOC 2 evidence reconciliation. To avoid last-minute gaps during their audit window, we prefer to have a small set of critical artifacts placed in escrow (read-only, time-limited) so the security reviewers can access them without repeated downloads.

What we're asking for: a one-time upload of the items listed in the attached SIG-CAIQ-Refresh-Checklist.xlsx and the signed evidence escrow form (attached). Specific asks:
- CAIQ answers snapshot (export JSON or Excel)
- SOC 2: latest security monitoring configuration docs + redacted incident log for last 12 months
- Key SSO metadata (IdP SAML certs) and MFA policy screenshots
- Any compensating control narratives for data isolation we can reference

Preferred upload targets (pick one):
1) Vendr profile upload (recommended) — we will grant a time-limited uploader token and then lock the files into a read-only escrow folder.
2) S3 staging bucket (private link) — we will provide a pre-signed URL and verify checksum.

Timeline: can you get the bundle uploaded by EOD Tue, 25 May? Our audit window for the reviewer is 26–28 May and we want everything staged ahead of time.

Attachments: SIG-CAIQ-Refresh-Checklist.xlsx, evidence-escrow-request-form.pdf, vendr-upload-instructions.txt

Thanks — let me know if you need a short walkthrough.

—
Aditya Rao
Head of Sales, Enterprise | Redwood Inference
aditya@redwoodinference.com | (415) 555-0173


From: Jared Lin <jared@cloudco.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Sharma <priya.sharma@cloudco.com>, Kevin Ortiz <kevin.ortiz@procure.cloudco.com>
Date: Fri, 21 May 2027 10:03:12 -0700
Subject: Re: SIG / CAIQ annual refresh — request to escrow critical artifacts in Vendr

Aditya —

Thanks for the clear checklist. We can do this but need clarification on two items:
1) Escrow vs portal: our policy prefers the vendor portal (Vendr) for questionnaire answers but we typically push SOC2 artifacts to our S3 retention bucket for legal reasons. Will Redwood accept the S3 staging approach (pre-signed URL) for SOC2 PDFs only, while we upload CAIQ answers and the questionnaire snapshot to Vendr?
2) Redaction guidance: do you need the incident log fully redacted for customer PII or is a redacted summary sufficient?

We aim to have the CAIQ snapshot and questionnaire answers in Vendr by Tue 25 May; SOC2 PDFs might slip to Wed 26 May if we must run redaction through legal.

Thanks — Jared

title: SteelForge listing & integration exception — proposed plan
preview:
From: Asha Raman <asha.raman@steelforge.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 20 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Quick sync: marketplace listing + integration dependency

Ben —

Thanks again for the call this week. We scoped the technical work on our side and there are two items that will block the Cloud Marketplace pre-list gating:

- License entitlement verification flow (our SSO returns org.id only; marketplace needs tokenized entitlement proof)
- Deployment packaging: SteelForge delivers a containerized adaptor that needs a dedicated IAM role when deployed into the marketplace staging account

We want to understand if Redwood can grant a temporary entitlement exception for the early-ship, while we complete the entitlement flow (we estimate 4–6 weeks). Also need a short checklist of the security artifacts your marketplace team will require to approve packaging.

Can you connect me to the right folks? We're targeting a July GA and would prefer not to miss the marketplace window.

—Asha
Asha Raman
Head of Cloud Partnerships, SteelForge



From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>, Camila Reyes <camila_reyes@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 20 May 2027 09:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Quick sync: marketplace listing + integration dependency

Looping you all in —

SteelForge is asking for an entitlement exception and a packaging checklist. They want to ship early (targeting July GA) and have an adaptor requiring a dedicated IAM role. Can the marketplace/security path grant a short-term exception while they finish the tokenized entitlement?

Immediate asks:
- Karthik: what are the minimum gating checks for a temporary exception (network config, signing, runtime constraints)?
- Camila / Laura: product/partner side — will an exception break any entitlements/ratelimit assumptions or billing hooks?
- Marissa: FYI for marketplace listing timeline — do we have cadence to review exemptions?

I attached their scope doc and our exemption template.

Thanks —
Ben
Strategic Partnerships, Redwood Inference

Attachment: steelforge-integration-scope.pdf


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Camila Reyes <camila_reyes@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 21 May 2027 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Quick sync: marketplace listing + integration dependency

Ben / team —

Read their scope. Tech notes / constraints for an exception window:

1) Entitlement verification: we can accept an interim flow if SteelForge provides a signed assertion per user session (JSON Web Signature) referencing org.id + product_id. We'll need a documented verification endpoint and sample JWTs before staging deploy.

2) IAM role for adaptor: acceptable if the container runs in a sandboxed ephemeral account we control (marketplace-staging). We cannot accept third-party-managed roles that elevate beyond scoped S3 / KMS access. Need a minimal IAM policy proposal from SteelForge.

3) Runtime observability: exception deployments must enable extended logs and trace hooks for 30 days so we can audit for false positives.

4) Security artifacts: dependency SBOM, current penetration test report (last 12 months), and SOC-related attestations if available.

If we get those items, I can sign off for a limited 45-day exception window with two checkpoints (day 14 security review, day 30 production-readiness check). Otherwise, we should hold to the normal gating.

Karthik
Senior Infra Engineer — Serving Runtime



From: Camila Reyes <camila_reyes@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 24 May 2027 08:20:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Quick sync: marketplace listing + integration dependency

Thanks — high level product considerations:

- Billing/entitlements: if we accept signed assertions for the interim, we need a mapping layer to reconcile seats/usage with our billing pipeline. That adds a manual reconciliation step; finance needs to be aware to prevent revenue leakage.
- Feature toggles: we'll gate any auth-bypassing exception behind a feature flag so the rest of traffic remains unaffected. Ops needs a rollback plan.
- Support SLAs: SteelForge must agree to an elevated incident runbook and a dedicated contact during the exception window.

I can commit product-side to these if legal signs off on the temporary terms and finance approves an invoicing reconciliation process. Recommend we package terms in a 1–page MOU for the 45-day window.

Camila Reyes\Director, Product Partnerships

Attachment: marketplace-exemption-request.docx


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Asha Raman <asha.raman@steelforge.ai>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>, Camila Reyes <camila_reyes@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 24 May 2027 14:55:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Quick sync: marketplace listing + integration dependency

Asha —

Thanks for the clear ask. Internal summary and proposed path:

1) Redwood can approve a 45-day entitlement exception conditioned on:
   - SteelForge provides sample signed JWT assertions and a verification endpoint (dev/staging)
   - Minimal IAM policy for their adaptor scoped to marketplace-staging (we'll provide a template)
   - SBOM + penetration test report and a named engineering on-call for the exception window
   - Enable extended logs/tracing for 30 days (our infra team will share collection hooks)

2) Product & Finance: we'll run a manual reconciliation for billing during the window; Camila will own the MOU and finance handoff.

3) Timeline: if SteelForge provides artifacts by 5/31 we can target a staging deploy the week of 6/7 and listing review the week of 6/14. Final GA dependent on remediation items.

Next steps for you:
- Reply with a short timeline and a point-of-contact for devops + security.
- Confirm you can produce the sample JWTs and the adaptor IAM policy by 5/31.

We'll follow up with the formal exemption MOU and the marketplace checklist links.

Best —
Ben
Strategic Partnerships, Redwood Inference

—
Ben Carter
Strategic Partnerships, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0192

title: RFI: Cross-region retention, exportability and restore operations
preview:
From: Jane Doe <jane.doe@finco.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: security-gov@finco.com
Date: 2027-05-21T09:12:00Z
Subject: RFI: Cross-region retention, exportability and restore operations

Hi Ava,

Thanks for participating in our RFP. A couple of follow-ups from our legal and security teams around logging/audit retention and exportability:

1) Retention windows: what retention buckets do you support (default and customizable options)? Can retention be set per-tenant, per-route, or per-index?

2) Cross-region retention/replication: if we require data residency in multiple regions (us-east-1 and eu-west-1), how do you guarantee exportability of the full audit trail for a given tenant and time range?

3) Bulk export & restore SLA: expected timelines to produce a bulk extract (raw audit events + manifests) and turnaround for a restore into our environment. Any expedited options?

4) Chain of custody & tamper evidence: what artifacts do you provide to show integrity of an export (checksums, signed manifests, notarization?).

5) Encryption & KMS: do you support customer-managed keys for exported archives?

We need these answers for the compliance appendix. If you have a sample runbook or manifest format you can attach, that would be helpful.

Regards,
Jane Doe
Senior Information Security Manager
FinCo Bank

[Attachment requested: sample export manifest and runbook]


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Jane Doe <jane.doe@finco.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-05-21T13:45:00Z
Subject: Re: RFI: Cross-region retention, exportability and restore operations

Jane — thanks for the clear list. Quick high-level answers up front; Rishi/Kim and I are attaching a short runbook and a manifest example and can follow with a technical call.

1) Retention windows
- Default: 90 days of hot audit events, 365 days of indexable audit history in cold tier.
- Configurable tiers: 90d / 1y / 3y / 7y / custom multi-year policies available via contract for dedicated/private deployments.
- Scope: retention can be set at tenant-level and route-level (e.g., admin actions vs runtime requests). Per-object retention (individual ID) is supported via retention tags and legal-hold flags.

2) Cross-region replication & exportability
- Architecture: primary write region + configurable async replication to one or more replica regions. Replication uses an append-only journal with sequence IDs and monotonic timestamps to preserve ordering across regions.
- Exportability: we provide a deterministic bulk extract that replays the journal for a requested tenant/time-range. Export keys into the manifest map to original region/replica indexes so you can reconstruct ordering and provenance.

3) Bulk export & restore SLA
- Standard (included): 48–72 hours to produce a full bulk extract (up to 2TB). Delivery via customer-specified S3 bucket (pre-signed, time-limited PUT), or direct SFTP transfer.
- Expedited (add-on): 8–12 hours for up to 1TB with committed bandwidth — available under Dedicated/Private plans or one-off fee for Hosted customers.
- Restore assistance: we provide a documented restore procedure and can perform a turn-key restore to a customer-owned environment under Professional Services (timeline depends on dataset size; typical 24–72 hours).

4) Chain of custody & tamper evidence
- Export package includes: NDJSON event shards, a manifest.csv (with event counts, shard checksums SHA-256, sequence ID ranges), and a signed package summary.
- Signing: manifests are PGP-signed by Redwood's export service; optionally we can provide a notarized hash timestamp (third-party timestamping for legal workflows) under enterprise contract.

5) Encryption & KMS
- At rest: all exported artifacts are encrypted at rest in our object storage (AES-256).
- In transit: exports are delivered over TLS.
- BYOK: we support customer-managed keys (AWS KMS/HSM) for exported archives for Dedicated and Private deployments; for Hosted we offer envelope encryption with customer-owned KMS integration on contract.

Attachments: runbook (redwood_bulk_export_runbook_v1.pdf), example manifest (export_manifest_example.ndjson), chain-of-custody template (chain_of_custody_template.xlsx).

Action: can you confirm the retention term(s) you need and whether you want expedited export SLAs included in the proposal? I can schedule a 45–minute walkthrough with Rishi to run through a sample export flow.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Senior Sales Engineer
Redwood Inference

[Attachment: redwood_bulk_export_runbook_v1.pdf, export_manifest_example.ndjson, chain_of_custody_template.xlsx]


From: Jane Doe <jane.doe@finco.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: security-gov@finco.com
Date: 2027-05-22T08:30:00Z
Subject: Re: RFI: Cross-region retention, exportability and restore operations

Ava — appreciate the thoroughs. A couple of follow-ups after internal review:

- BYOK: we will require customer-managed KMS for all archive handoffs. Is there a technical pre-req (bucket ACLs, IAM role trust) you need from our side?
- Scoping: exports must be filterable to a set of customer_subIDs and arbitrary date ranges. Confirmed?
- Redaction: can exported events be post-processed to redact PII fields (names, SSNs) prior to delivery, or do we need to handle redaction after receipt?
- Evidence: for legal hold cases we need an un-redacted notarized package plus a separate redacted package for operational teams. Can you produce both in the same SLA window?

Also please confirm whether hashes in the manifest are SHA-256 and whether the PGP signatures include a timestamp. Finally, can you attach a sample S3 manifest with a short header we can share with Legal?

If everything looks good we'll ask Procurement to include the expedited export add-on in the SOW.

Regards,
Jane

On Fri, May 21, 2027 at 13:45 Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Jane — thanks for the clear list. Quick high-level answers up front; Rishi/Kim and I are attaching a short runbook and a manifest example and can follow with a technical call.
> 
> [quoted content omitted]


From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
To: Jane Doe <jane.doe@finco.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-05-23T11:05:00Z
Subject: Re: RFI: Cross-region retention, exportability and restore operations

Jane — following up with granular confirmations and the requested artifacts attached. TL;DR: yes to BYOK + scoping + dual-package (notarized + redacted) in the expedited SLA window with pre-arranged PS support. Details below.

- BYOK / Delivery prerequisites:
  * We support AWS KMS (CMKs) and customer-managed HSM. For S3 handoffs you must provide a write-role ARN for a pre-signed bucket or accept a pre-signed PUT flow. We will exchange a short IAM trust policy during onboarding; minimal permissions: s3:PutObject, s3:PutObjectAcl (for delivery verification), kms:Encrypt for envelope operations if you retain CMK control.

- Scoping and filters:
  * Exports can be filtered by tenant_id, sub_customer_id list, event_type, and arbitrary start/end timestamps. We also support queryable include/exclude filters for meta-fields.

- Redaction & dual-package workflow:
  * Pipeline: export -> optional automated redaction pass (configurable regex/field maps) -> generate unredacted package + manifest -> PGP-sign + notarize unredacted hash -> generate redacted package (separate shards) -> PGP-sign redacted manifest.
  * Time budget: for expedited exports (<=1TB) we can produce both packages within the 8–12 hour SLA if redaction rules are pre-profiled during onboarding. For larger data sets PS engagement will provide a scoped timeline.

- Integrity artifacts:
  * Checksums: SHA-256 for each shard (stored in manifest.csv).
  * Signature: PGP signature block with RFC3161 timestamping available as an option.

- Restore mechanics:
  * We provide a step-by-step restore runbook (attached) that maps shard ranges to replay commands. For legal-hold restores we can also provide a notarized chain-of-custody PDF and cross-checksums.

Attachments included: redwood_bulk_export_runbook_v1.pdf, export_manifest_example.ndjson, chain_of_custody_template.xlsx.

Next step: Ava will schedule a 45-minute export walkthrough with your SecOps team. Please share two 1-hour windows next week (May 31–June 2) and confirm whether Procurement is proceeding with the expedited-add-on in the SOW.

Best,
Rishi Malhotra
Principal Security Engineer, Redwood Inference

[Attachments: redwood_bulk_export_runbook_v1.pdf, export_manifest_example.ndjson, chain_of_custody_template.xlsx]

title: Cross-region offer floor escalation - Bengaluru candidate
preview:
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 20 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Cross-region offer floor escalation - Bengaluru candidate

Hi team,

Following up from our loop yesterday about the Senior ML Engineer candidate in Bengaluru (Priya Singh). Recruiting is seeing a hard market locally and the candidate has a competing offer from a mature startup that's ~12% above the standard US-equivalent offer we typically bring for this role. The candidate is strong (profile attached) and Karthik believes immediate replacement risk is medium-high.

Request: can we approve a one-time cross-region adjustment to the base offer for this hire? Proposed options:
- Option A (recommended): bump base by 12% relative to the standard band (effectively move to top of band + COLA uplift).
- Option B: keep base within band but add a 12% sign-on bonus paid 50% on start / 50% after 6 months.

Impact (high-level):
- Annual run-rate delta (Option A): +12% on comp budget for this headcount (~USD 13.2k incremental run-rate, see attached calc).
- One-time cost (Option B): sign-on bonus ~USD 16k (no run-rate impact).

Why now:
- Candidate has given a soft deadline (end of next week). Recruiting flagged retention risk if we stretch the timeline.
- This hire is for a product-critical role supporting latency optimization work; delaying is likely to push timelines on the model-serving roadmap.
